title: Synthetic voice grandparent scam — grandson in jail
summary: Caller used AI voice cloning to impersonate victim's grandson, claiming arrest and needing $2,400 bail. Caller said 'don't tell mom, she'll panic'. Victim wired money before family verified.
outcome: Victim lost $2,400. Local police confirmed voice cloning technology used.

title: Fake attorney grandparent scam
summary: Scammer called posing as a lawyer, claiming victim's grandson had been in a car accident and needed $5,000 for legal fees immediately. Insisted on secrecy to 'not upset the family'.
outcome: Victim lost $5,000 via wire transfer before contacting grandson directly.

title: Grandparent scam via text — fake hospital
summary: SMS claiming to be from a hospital on behalf of victim's daughter, requesting $800 emergency deposit to a Zelle account. Message said 'your daughter can't call right now'.
outcome: Victim sent $800 to unknown Zelle before verifying. Daughter was safe at home.

title: Voice-cloned child emergency scam
summary: AI-generated voice of son called father claiming to be stranded abroad after robbery. Requested $3,200 sent to foreign account. Told father 'don't tell Mom, she'll worry'.
outcome: Father lost $3,200. Son was in office when call occurred.

title: Fake bail bondsman family scam
summary: Caller claimed to be bail bondsman for grandson arrested on drug charges. Required $1,500 cash sent via courier immediately. Threatened grandson would spend night in jail without payment.
outcome: Victim lost $1,500. No arrest had occurred.

title: Romance-to-family-impersonation hybrid
summary: Scammer built trust over weeks then claimed to be victim's long-lost relative in crisis, needing $10,000 for medical emergency. Sent fabricated hospital documents.
outcome: Victim lost $10,000 over two wire transfers.

title: Grandson car accident scam with courier pickup
summary: Classic grandparent scam: caller claimed to be grandson in accident, sent courier to collect $4,000 cash from victim's home. Told victim not to hang up during entire process.
outcome: Victim lost $4,000 cash handed to courier.

title: Synthetic voice daughter kidnapping scam
summary: AI-cloned voice of daughter screamed for help, then 'kidnapper' demanded $50,000 ransom. Told husband not to call police and to stay on phone. Daughter was at work unharmed.
outcome: Victim nearly wired $50,000. Wife called daughter's work and stopped transfer.

title: Social media info used in grandparent scam
summary: Scammer scraped grandson's Facebook for details (college, friends' names) to make fake call convincing. Said 'it's me Jake from State U, I got arrested at a party'.
outcome: Victim nearly wired $6,000. Saved by calling grandson's roommate.

title: Multi-call grandparent scheme
summary: First call: fake grandson. Second call: fake police officer. Third call: fake judge demanding $3,000 court fee. Each call escalated pressure and reinforced secrecy.
outcome: Victim lost $3,000 before neighbor intervened.

title: Korean prosecutor phone scam
summary: Caller claimed to be prosecutor investigating money laundering linked to victim's bank account. Demanded victim transfer all funds to 'government safe account' for protection. Threatened immediate arrest if uncooperative.
outcome: Victim lost $45,000 in multiple wire transfers over three days.

title: IRS agent impersonation arrest threat
summary: Robocall followed by live agent claiming to be IRS criminal investigation. Said arrest warrant issued for tax fraud. Demanded gift cards to settle immediately.
outcome: Victim purchased $3,000 in Google Play gift cards before family intervened.

title: FBI impersonation online crime scam
summary: Email and follow-up call from supposed FBI agent claiming victim's IP address was linked to child exploitation. Demanded $10,000 to avoid public arrest. Told victim to keep matter private.
outcome: Victim wired $10,000 before son discovered fraud.

title: Medicare fraud investigator scam
summary: Caller posing as Medicare fraud investigator said victim's SSN was used in $90,000 fraud. Required 'asset verification' — victim's bank account details — to clear their name.
outcome: Victim provided account details; $12,000 drained within hours.

title: Social Security Administration suspension scam
summary: Automated call claimed SSN was suspended due to suspicious activity in Texas. Press 1 to speak with officer. Live operator demanded wire transfer to 'protect assets'.
outcome: Victim lost $8,500 via cashier's check.

title: Police officer drug money scam
summary: Caller said they were a detective; victim's bank account flagged for drug money. Required victim to withdraw all cash and hand to undercover officer to 'investigate the bank'.
outcome: Victim withdrew $22,000 and handed to fake officer.

title: DEA impersonation cryptocurrency scam
summary: Text claiming to be DEA agent said victim's identity was used in a drug shipment. Required cryptocurrency payment to 'government wallet' to avoid arrest.
outcome: Victim sent $5,000 in Bitcoin.

title: CEO wire fraud BEC attack
summary: Email spoofing CEO to CFO: urgent vendor payment of $87,500 needed same day. CEO claimed to be in board meeting and unavailable for calls. New bank account in email body.
outcome: Company lost $87,500 before IT flagged domain mismatch.

title: Real estate closing BEC interception
summary: Attacker compromised title company email, sent buyer fake wire instructions for $340,000 home purchase closing. Looked identical to real closing emails.
outcome: Buyer wired $340,000 to fraudulent account. Funds unrecoverable.

title: Vendor email compromise payroll redirect
summary: HR received email from 'employee' requesting payroll direct deposit change to new bank account. Email came from slightly misspelled domain. Approved without callback verification.
outcome: Two payroll cycles sent to fraudulent account; company lost $24,000.

title: M&A deal BEC interception
summary: Law firm handling acquisition received spoofed email from client requesting final wire of $2.1M to 'updated escrow account'. Sent day before official closing.
outcome: $2.1M wired to criminal account. Partial recovery via FBI REACT team.

title: CFO impersonation W-2 data request
summary: HR manager received email appearing to be from CFO requesting all employee W-2 forms 'for board audit'. Sent all 847 employee records to fraudulent address.
outcome: All employee tax data compromised. Used in identity theft campaign.

title: Construction company invoice redirect
summary: Contractor's email compromised; invoices sent to clients had payment instructions replaced with fraudulent account. Clients paid for 3 weeks before discovering fraud.
outcome: Four clients lost total of $198,000.

title: Nonprofit donation redirect BEC
summary: Nonprofit's email hacked; major donor received 'updated wire instructions' for $150,000 annual gift. Domain was one character off from real domain.
outcome: Donor wired $150,000 to fraudulent account.

title: IT vendor impersonation invoice scam
summary: Finance team received email from 'IT vendor' with updated banking info for quarterly invoice. Phone number in email went to scammer. Verified by calling number in email instead of original contract.
outcome: Company paid $43,000 to fraudulent account.

title: Military romance scam cryptocurrency
summary: Facebook profile of US Army officer stationed overseas. Built 3-month relationship then requested $8,000 for 'medical emergency' via cryptocurrency. Refused video calls claiming poor internet.
outcome: Victim sent $8,000 in Bitcoin. Profile photos stolen from real soldier.

title: Oil rig engineer romance scam
summary: Widowed victim met 'petroleum engineer' on dating app. After months of messaging, engineer needed $12,000 for equipment emergency to get home and 'start life together'.
outcome: Victim lost $12,000 over three transfers.

title: Pig butchering crypto investment romance
summary: Match introduced victim to 'cryptocurrency investment platform' after building romantic relationship. Small initial profit encouraged larger $95,000 investment. Withdrawal blocked by 'tax fees'.
outcome: Victim lost $95,000 attempting to withdraw 'profits'.

title: Widower doctor romance scam
summary: Profile of widowed doctor with daughter on Facebook. After six weeks of messaging, needed $6,500 for daughter's school fees while waiting for hospital paycheck to clear.
outcome: Victim sent $6,500. Same photos matched to multiple fraud reports.

title: Instagram model romance gift card scam
summary: Attractive Instagram profile reached out, built relationship, then needed $2,000 in Amazon gift cards for 'visa processing fees' to visit victim.
outcome: Victim purchased $2,000 in gift cards before friend intervened.

title: Crypto romance scam with fake exchange
summary: Online match who claimed to be a successful trader showed victim 'earnings' on a fake exchange platform. Victim deposited $50,000 total; platform became inaccessible overnight.
outcome: Victim lost $50,000. Exchange domain registered 2 weeks before scam.

title: USPS redelivery SMS phishing
summary: SMS: 'USPS: Your package needs address confirmation. Pay $3.50 redelivery fee at usps-delivery-confirm.net'. Link led to fake USPS site harvesting credit card data.
outcome: Multiple victims provided credit card data. Fraudulent charges followed.

title: FedEx package held SMS scam
summary: 'FedEx: Package held at facility. Click to reschedule: fedex-delivery-update.com'. Fake page requested full credit card and billing address for '$1.99 customs fee'.
outcome: Thousands of cards harvested in single campaign. CC fraud followed.

title: Amazon package problem phishing
summary: Email: 'Your Amazon order cannot be delivered. Verify your address at amazon-delivery-verify.com'. Collected Amazon login credentials and credit card.
outcome: Amazon accounts compromised; fraudulent orders placed.

title: DHL customs fee text scam
summary: 'DHL: International package held. Pay £2.99 customs duty: dhl-customs-fee.co.uk'. Redirected to convincing fake page capturing card details.
outcome: Victims charged recurring subscriptions from captured card data.

title: UPS address verification phishing email
summary: HTML email mimicking UPS design: 'Click to update delivery address within 24 hours or package returned'. Link went to ups-track-delivery.net collecting PII and CC.
outcome: Personal data harvested and sold on dark web marketplace.

title: Fake toll authority SMS phishing
summary: 'EZPass: Unpaid toll of $3.75. Avoid collections: pay at ezpass-toll-account.com'. High volume campaign targeting multiple states.
outcome: Tens of thousands of cards compromised. FBI issued public warning.

title: Bank account suspended phishing text
summary: 'Chase: Unusual activity detected. Your account is suspended. Verify at chase-secure-verify.com'. Fake page captured username, password, and one-time code.
outcome: Account drained within minutes of credential capture.

title: Wells Fargo Zelle reversal scam
summary: Text: 'Wells Fargo: Unusual Zelle payment of $850 detected. Cancel at wellsfargo-fraud-center.com'. Victim called number in text, gave account credentials to fake fraud center.
outcome: Victim's account emptied via same-day Zelle transfers.

title: Bank of America new device phishing
summary: Email: 'A new device has accessed your account. Not you? Verify at bofa-secure.com'. Collected login and 2FA code in real time to drain account before victim discovered.
outcome: $23,000 withdrawn via ATM and wire within 30 minutes.

title: Crypto ATM bank impersonation scam
summary: Caller claimed to be bank fraud department; convinced victim to deposit cash at Bitcoin ATM to 'protect funds' during investigation. Sent fake badge photo via text.
outcome: Victim deposited $17,000 at crypto ATMs over two days.

title: Citibank OTP interception phishing
summary: SMS: 'Citibank: Your account will be closed. Verify now: citi-account-restore.com'. Fake site prompted for username, password, then forwarded real OTP in real time.
outcome: Session hijack enabled $31,000 in fraudulent transfers.

title: Fake overdraft fee bank text scam
summary: Text from 'your bank': 'Overdraft fee of $35 pending. Dispute at bank-overdraft-dispute.com'. Collected full card number, CVV, expiry.
outcome: Cards used for international purchases within hours.

title: Google account security alert phishing
summary: Email: 'Google: Someone signed in from Russia. Secure your account now: google-accounts-verify.com'. Harvested Google credentials used for account takeover.
outcome: Email accounts compromised, used for BEC attacks on victim's contacts.

title: Microsoft 365 credential phishing
summary: Email with Microsoft logo: 'Your Microsoft 365 license expires today. Renew at microsoft-365-renewal.com'. Credential harvesting page with realistic design.
outcome: Corporate credentials harvested, ransomware deployed 48 hours later.

title: IRS tax refund phishing email
summary: 'IRS: You have a pending refund of $2,847. Claim at irs-refund-portal.com within 72 hours'. Collected SSN, date of birth, and bank account for 'direct deposit'.
outcome: Identity theft and fraudulent tax returns filed using harvested data.

title: Netflix account suspended phishing
summary: 'Netflix: Your payment failed. Update billing at netflix-account-update.com'. Collected credit card with realistic Netflix-themed page.
outcome: Card used for $3,200 in unauthorized purchases.

title: Healthcare portal credential phishing
summary: SMS: 'Your MyChart test results are ready. View at mychart-health-results.com'. Harvested healthcare portal credentials and PII.
outcome: Health records accessed; identity theft for prescription fraud.

title: DocuSign phishing for contract signing
summary: Email mimicking DocuSign: 'Sign your contract by end of day: docusign-contract-sign.net'. Collected Microsoft or Google credentials via fake SSO page.
outcome: Corporate SSO credentials harvested, used in follow-up BEC.

title: Venmo payment notification phishing
summary: Text: 'You received $340 on Venmo. Claim at venmo-payment-claim.com'. Fake claim page collected Venmo login, then harvested linked bank account.
outcome: Venmo account accessed; linked bank account drained $2,100.

title: PayPal account limitation phishing
summary: 'PayPal: Your account has been limited. Restore access at paypal-account-restore.com within 24 hours'. Collected PayPal credentials and linked card details.
outcome: PayPal account used for $4,500 in purchases before victim noticed.

title: LinkedIn job offer phishing
summary: LinkedIn message with link to 'apply for high-paying remote role': linkedin-job-apply.com. Collected resume, SSN, and bank account for 'payroll setup'.
outcome: Identity used to open fraudulent credit lines.

title: Apple ID suspended phishing
summary: 'Apple: Your Apple ID has been locked. Verify at apple-id-verify.com'. Collected Apple credentials, used to access iCloud and lock victim's devices for ransom.
outcome: Device locked; victim paid $200 in iTunes gift cards for unlock code.

title: HR benefits enrollment phishing
summary: Internal-looking email: 'Open enrollment closes today. Update your benefits at hr-enrollment-portal.com'. Targeted employees with corporate-branded page, collected SSO credentials.
outcome: Multiple employee credentials harvested; payroll diverted.

title: Tax document W-2 phishing
summary: 'Your W-2 is available for download: payroll-docs-secure.com'. Professional-looking page collected employee ID and PIN for corporate payroll system.
outcome: Payroll credentials used to redirect direct deposits.

title: Zoom meeting invite phishing
summary: Email: 'You missed a Zoom meeting. Review recording at zoom-meeting-record.com'. Collected Microsoft 365 credentials via fake sign-in prompt.
outcome: Email account compromised and used to launch spear phishing campaign.

title: COVID relief fund phishing
summary: Text: 'You qualify for a $1,400 COVID relief payment. Apply at covid-relief-gov.com'. Collected full name, SSN, bank routing, and account number.
outcome: Identity used to file fraudulent tax returns and open credit cards.

title: Student loan forgiveness phishing
summary: 'Your student loans qualify for forgiveness. Apply before deadline: studentloan-relief.gov.com'. Collected FSA ID credentials and bank account for 'refund processing'.
outcome: FSA accounts accessed; fraudulent loan applications submitted.

title: Cryptocurrency investment romance scam
summary: Victim met 'financial analyst' on Hinge. After weeks of building trust, she showed victim how to invest in 'insider crypto trades' on a fake platform. Deposits totaled $180,000.
outcome: Victim lost $180,000. Platform disappeared with all funds.

title: Deployed soldier gift card romance scam
summary: 'US soldier in Syria' on Facebook needed $1,200 in gift cards for 'satellite phone minutes' to call victim. Used real soldier's photos from public military Facebook page.
outcome: Victim sent $1,200 in Best Buy gift cards.

title: Widower businessman romance scam
summary: Widowed businessman on Match.com needed help with 'export business emergency': $15,000 loan to be repaid after deal closed. Built 4-month relationship before asking.
outcome: Victim wired $15,000. Man's profile used stolen executive photos.

title: Tech support grandparent scam
summary: Pop-up claimed computer hacked, required calling number. Support agent said bank account compromised and demanded $10,000 cash be handed to 'security agent'. Told to keep secret from family.
outcome: Victim handed $10,000 cash to stranger at coffee shop.

title: Treasury Department money laundering scam
summary: Caller claimed to be Treasury Department agent; victim's SSN linked to money laundering in Mexico. Required asset transfer to 'government-protected accounts' for 30-day investigation.
outcome: Victim transferred $67,000 over one week.

title: Fake fraud prevention call bank scam
summary: Inbound call from spoofed bank number. Agent said account being emptied in real time, needed victim to move funds to 'safe account' immediately.
outcome: Victim transferred $28,000 to fraudulent account.

title: Customs clearance cryptocurrency scam
summary: Email: 'Your international package requires $240 customs clearance. Pay via Bitcoin only: customs-clearance-btc.com'. Package did not exist.
outcome: Multiple victims sent cryptocurrency for non-existent packages.

title: School emergency fee BEC scam
summary: Email appearing to come from school principal requested urgent $400 iTunes gift cards for 'student field trip emergency'. Reply-to address was different from display sender.
outcome: Multiple parents each sent $400 in gift cards.

title: Online game romance scam
summary: Player in online game built romantic relationship over months. Eventually claimed to be injured and hospitalized in Philippines, needed $3,500 for surgery they couldn't afford.
outcome: Victim wired $3,500. Player was a scam operation with multiple simultaneous victims.

title: Warrant clearance police impersonation
summary: Text and follow-up call claiming active arrest warrant for missed jury duty. Could be cleared by paying $1,800 in gift cards immediately to avoid public arrest.
outcome: Victim sent $1,800 in Walmart gift cards.

title: Mobile banking app fake SMS scam
summary: SMS mimicking bank's real shortcode number: 'Debit card blocked for security. Re-enable at usbank-card-restore.com'. Collected card number, expiry, CVV, and online banking password.
outcome: Card used immediately for $4,200 in online purchases.

title: Emergency hospital call grandparent scam
summary: Caller claimed to be hospital nurse; granddaughter unconscious from accident. 'Attorney' called next, required $3,500 to 'release medical hold'. Told victim not to worry other family members yet.
outcome: Victim wired $3,500. Granddaughter was unharmed at home.

title: HR benefits manager impersonation
summary: Email chain appearing to be from HR director to payroll: 'Update John Smith's direct deposit per his request attached'. Attachment was fake form with fraudulent account.
outcome: One month's salary ($8,400) sent to fraudulent account.

title: Tax preparer portal phishing
summary: 'Your tax preparer shared documents via SecureFilePro. View: taxdocs-securefile.com'. Collected TurboTax or H&R Block credentials and SSN.
outcome: Tax accounts used to redirect refunds to fraudulent bank accounts.

title: SHEIN order problem phishing
summary: 'SHEIN: Your order is on hold. Verify payment at shein-order-verify.com'. Targeted young shoppers, collected card details with gift card offer as bait.
outcome: Cards used for subscription fraud.

title: Instagram influencer romance crypto scam
summary: Instagram DM from attractive profile claiming to be a crypto trader. Built weeks of trust, then invited victim to invest on fake platform showing real-time fake returns.
outcome: Victim deposited $22,000 before platform vanished.

title: Mortgage refinancing phishing email
summary: Email: 'Your mortgage servicer has updated payment instructions. Pay at newrez-payment-portal.com'. Timed to match victim's actual payment cycle.
outcome: Mortgage payment of $2,100 sent to fraudulent account.

title: Impersonation via cloned number
summary: Caller ID showed daughter's actual saved number (spoofed). Said she was in car accident and new account needed for tow and hospital payment. Victim trusted immediately due to caller ID.
outcome: Victim sent $2,200 before calling daughter's work directly.

title: ICE immigration enforcement scam
summary: Robocall to immigrant community: 'ICE has a removal order for your address. Pay $2,500 immediately to cancel'. Targeted Spanish speakers with fake ICE agent.
outcome: Multiple victims paid $2,500 each.

title: Freelancer payment redirect scam
summary: Email to client appeared to be from freelancer's domain (one letter off): 'Please use updated payment details for this invoice'. Client updated payment info without verifying.
outcome: $12,500 in invoices paid to fraudulent account.

title: Two-factor authentication bypass phishing
summary: Sophisticated phishing kit using real-time relay: victim entered credentials on fake bank page, scammer logged into real bank simultaneously, prompted for OTP which victim entered on fake page.
outcome: Account drained while victim was on phishing page.

title: Gift card balance check phishing
summary: 'Check your Amazon gift card balance at amazon-gift-balance.com'. Scraped gift card codes from balance check requests, drained cards before victims used them.
outcome: Hundreds of gift card balances drained.

title: Royal Mail redelivery phishing UK
summary: 'Royal Mail: Delivery failed. Pay £1.99 to reschedule: royalmail-redelivery.co'. Harvested card details with convincing Royal Mail branding.
outcome: Major UK phishing campaign — tens of thousands of victims.

title: Accounts payable new vendor scam
summary: Email to AP team: 'Our accounting firm is migrating banking. Please update to new ACH details for all future invoices'. No callback verification performed.
outcome: Three monthly payments totaling $67,000 sent to fraudulent account.

title: WhatsApp new number grandparent scam
summary: WhatsApp message: 'Hi Mom, I broke my phone and this is my new number. I have an urgent bill of $600 I need today — can you send via bank transfer?' Sender had profile photo of victim's child.
outcome: Victim transferred £600 before calling son.

title: Nephew overseas emergency scam
summary: Call from 'nephew' traveling in Mexico: robbed at gunpoint, needs $2,000 wire to hotel or they won't release his passport. Don't tell parents yet.
outcome: Victim wired $2,000. Nephew was at home in Ohio.

title: Stimulus check QR code phishing
summary: Mailed letter with official-looking IRS branding and QR code: 'Scan to claim your $1,400 Economic Impact Payment'. QR led to phishing site collecting SSN and bank details.
outcome: Identity theft and fraudulent direct deposit changes.

title: Investment account wire fraud via phone
summary: Caller claiming to be Fidelity fraud team: large suspicious wire being processed now. To stop it, victim must initiate a counter-wire of their own funds to 'isolated holding account'.
outcome: Victim wired $95,000 retirement savings.

title: Expired passport border agency scam
summary: Automated message: 'US Customs has flagged your passport. Call 1-800-XXX immediately to avoid detainment at border'. Live agent demanded wire payment to clear record.
outcome: Victim wired $4,500.

title: Missed court summons delivery scam
summary: Door hanger appeared to be from US Marshals: 'You missed delivery of court summons. Call to avoid arrest'. Call led to demand for $800 in gift cards to reschedule.
outcome: Victim sent $800 in gift cards.

title: Catfish check bouncing romance scam
summary: Online partner sent large check as 'investment in their relationship', asked victim to deposit and wire back $3,000 'for expenses'. Check bounced after wire sent.
outcome: Victim out $3,000 when check bounced.

title: Card verification scam text
summary: Text from 'bank': 'New card issued to your account. If you did not request this, reply STOP or visit capitalone-card-verify.com'. Credential harvesting at fake page.
outcome: Account credentials and 2FA code captured in real time.

title: CEO gift card request scam
summary: Email appeared to come from company CEO to assistant: 'I'm in a confidential meeting, can you quietly purchase 5x $500 Apple gift cards and send me the codes? I'll explain later.'
outcome: Assistant purchased $2,500 in gift cards and sent codes.

title: Long-distance relationship medical scam
summary: Six-month online relationship with 'doctor in Nigeria'. Emergency appendix surgery needed $4,000 or he would die. Sent photo of hospital bill.
outcome: Victim wired $4,000. Same script used on hundreds of victims.

title: QR code restaurant receipt phishing
summary: QR code on restaurant table: scammer replaced legitimate menu QR with malicious one linking to fake payment page that harvested card details.
outcome: Dozens of diners' card details stolen at multiple restaurants.

title: Elder financial abuse via reverse mortgage
summary: Caller posing as HUD counselor said victim's reverse mortgage was being audited. Required bank login to 'verify compliance'. Account then drained.
outcome: Victim's $43,000 home equity line drained.

title: M&A advisor spoofing wire fraud
summary: Investment banker received email from 'client' with slight domain variation requesting $1.8M wire to 'close deal today'. No callback policy in place.
outcome: $1.8M wired to Hong Kong account.

title: Job offer application data harvesting
summary: Fake indeed.com job posting for remote data entry position. Application form at jobs-apply-now.com collected SSN, driver's license photo, and bank details for 'direct deposit setup'.
outcome: Full identity package sold on dark web for $75-$150.

title: Spoofed police non-emergency line scam
summary: Caller ID showed local police non-emergency number. 'Officer' said grandson arrested for DUI and needs $1,500 bail processed discretely before appearing in court records.
outcome: Victim sent $1,500 Venmo to unknown account.

title: Grant disbursement BEC fraud
summary: Nonprofit finance director received email from apparent grant foundation: 'Update your ACH information to receive your $50,000 grant disbursement'. New account captured funds.
outcome: $50,000 grant routed to fraudulent account.

title: Redelivery gift card payment scam
summary: Missed delivery notice left at door: 'Pay $6.99 customs via iTunes gift card to 1-800-XXX'. Call center pressured victim to buy more cards for 'warehouse storage fees'.
outcome: Victim sent $400 in gift cards for non-existent package.

title: Email account recovery phishing
summary: 'Google: Someone requested account recovery for your email. Verify it's you: google-account-recovery.net'. Harvested password and recovery phone number.
outcome: Email account taken over; used for BEC and further phishing.

title: Jury duty failure warrant scam
summary: Text message: 'You failed to appear for jury duty. A bench warrant has been issued. Call immediately to avoid arrest'. Demanded $500 to clear warrant.
outcome: Victim sent $500 in Walmart gift cards.

title: Fake law enforcement passport hold
summary: Call to elderly couple: their son's passport held at JFK for unpaid customs fees of $3,200 on 'business equipment'. Son couldn't call as he was 'in customs processing'. Send Western Union immediately.
outcome: Couple sent $3,200. Son was in his office in Seattle.

title: Family grocery money request
summary: Adult child texted parent asking for $40 for groceries, offering to repay Sunday. Normal family financial communication with known contact, small amount, specific repayment plan.
outcome: Legitimate request. No scam activity.

title: Dentist appointment reminder
summary: Dentist office sent automated reminder for appointment tomorrow, including office phone number to confirm or cancel. Standard business reminder, known sender.
outcome: Legitimate reminder. No scam activity.

title: Amazon shipping notification
summary: Amazon sent order shipped notification with tracking number from amazon.com domain. No action required, links go to official amazon.com.
outcome: Legitimate notification. No scam activity.

title: Work meeting reschedule
summary: Manager sent Slack message moving Monday standup from 9am to 10am due to all-hands meeting. Known sender, no financial request, no suspicious link.
outcome: Legitimate work communication. No scam activity.

title: Venmo payment from friend
summary: Friend sent $25 via Venmo for their share of dinner last night with note 'for pizza'. From saved contact, no request to click any link, standard app notification.
outcome: Legitimate Venmo notification. No scam activity.

title: 2FA login code SMS
summary: SMS from bank's shortcode number: 'Your login code is 847291. Expires in 10 minutes. Never share this code.' User initiated the login themselves.
outcome: Legitimate 2FA code. No scam activity.

title: Credit card payment due reminder
summary: Chase sent official notification that payment is due in 3 days via chase.com domain email. Links go to official chase.com. No credential request.
outcome: Legitimate bank notification from official domain. No scam.

title: Child borrowing friend's phone
summary: Teen texted parent from friend's number to say practice ended early, asking for pickup. Explained whose phone they were using, no financial request.
outcome: Legitimate communication. Context explains new number.

title: Rent money request from adult child
summary: Adult son asked mom for $200 to cover rent since paycheck delayed by bank error, offering to repay Friday when check clears. From saved contact, specific reason, specific repayment date.
outcome: Legitimate family financial request. No scam indicators.

title: Doctor appointment follow-up
summary: Doctor's office called to follow up on test results and left voicemail with callback number matching their website. No financial request, no suspicious link.
outcome: Legitimate medical office communication. No scam.

title: Package delivered notification
summary: UPS text notification: 'Your package was delivered to front door.' No link to click, no action required, no payment request. Tracking number matches expected delivery.
outcome: Legitimate delivery notification. No scam activity.

title: Employer direct deposit notification
summary: Payroll system email from company HR domain: 'Your direct deposit of $2,450 has been processed for Friday.' From known corporate email, no action required.
outcome: Legitimate payroll notification. No scam activity.

title: Birthday greeting from friend
summary: Friend texted 'Happy birthday! Let's celebrate this weekend.' From saved contact, no financial request, no links, casual greeting.
outcome: Legitimate social message. No scam activity.

title: School event reminder
summary: School sent automated text about parent-teacher conference next week with office phone number and school website link (official school domain). Standard school communication.
outcome: Legitimate school communication. No scam activity.

title: Subscription renewal notice
summary: Netflix email from netflix.com domain: 'Your subscription renews on the 15th for $15.49.' No action required, link goes to netflix.com/account.
outcome: Legitimate subscription notice from official domain. No scam.
